infoAccordion.whatIsSurmaContent: Surma is a program designed for organizing fun assassination tournaments, maintained and developed by active members of the University of Helsinki assassination game players association, which is
infoAccordion.whatIsSurmaContent2: . Surma is used to manage assassination tournaments: players share information with each other through Surma during the tournament.
infoAccordion.whatIsAssassinationTournamentContent1: In an assassination tournament, players live their everyday lives while trying to get into situations unnoticed by others, where they can apply playful weapon substitutes to other players as allowed by the rules. The tournament is played among participants in murder chains: player A's goal is to assassinate the target, player B; player B hunts player C, and so on up to player N, who waits for player A. If A successfully kills B in a way allowed by the rules, A and B contact the referee and report what happened for scoring purposes. Now B drops out of the game, and A starts hunting C as their new target. The game is played around the clock until a predetermined time limit, at which point the game winner is determined by points. To make the game easier, players share, among other things, their calendars and home addresses with each other in Surma.
infoAccordion.whatIsAssassinationTournamentContent2: Participation in an assassination tournament begins by registering for the game through Surma, after which Surma and the tournament referee send additional instructions by email.
infoAccordion.whatIsAssassinationTournamentContent3: Read more about player data and how it is handled in
infoAccordion.whatIsAssassinationTournamentContent4: Read more about assassination tournaments and their rules on

userForm.title: Registration
userForm.welcomeMessage: Welcome to register for the HYS assassination tournament
userForm.description: Registration is a two-step process. First, we ask for your name, email address and phone number. This information is needed to log into Surma and for the referee to contact you about tournament-related matters. After confirming your email address, you will be able to enter the rest of the information required in the tournament. By registering for the tournament, you accept the University of Helsinki assassination players'
userForm.emailNotValidError: The provided email address is not valid. Try a different address
userForm.genericError: Something went wrong. Please contact the referee

playerForm.title: Registration
playerForm.description: This form asks for information relevant to the tournament, which will be visible to your hunters. After completing the form, the referee will confirm your registration.
playerForm.mandatoryFields: Only your alias and address are mandatory at this stage - you can fill in the other fields later and edit them during the tournament.
playerForm.calendarInfo: Calendar information should be kept up to date and sufficiently clear and detailed. You don't need to document every second, but the game becomes more fun for you and your hunters if you provide them with enough opportunities for assassination.
playerForm.imageUploadLabel: Choose a photo of yourself (max. 4 MB)
playerForm.imageReadError: Failed to read image file. Try another file
playerForm.imageUploadFailed: Failed to upload image to server
playerForm.safetyNotes: Safety notes (e.g. times and places outside the game)
playerForm.agreedTo: By registering for the tournament, you accept the University of Helsinki assassination players'
playerForm.error: Something went wrong. Please contact the referee
